commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an Overview-only AppKit hosting wrapper that keeps rich provider rows while moving row hover highlighting and hit testing out of the SwiftUI row tree.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence local reproduction was established in this PR review. The linked issue supplies a sample-backed macOS 27 path and current source still matches that path, but this branch has no after-fix live scroll profile.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs maintainer direction and live runtime proof, not an automated repair, because the remaining blocker is choosing and validating one of two mutually exclusive performance approaches.

target.title: Keep rich Overview rows but move hover to AppKit
target.body:
Refs #1674.

## Summary
- Add an Overview-only AppKit hosting wrapper for rich provider rows.
- Keep the current rich row content: header, usage section, storage text, submenu indicator, provider click behavior, and provider detail submenus.
- Move row hover highlight to a cheap `CALayer` on the AppKit boundary instead of propagating `menuItemHighlighted` through the full SwiftUI row tree.
- Preserve `menuCardRefreshMonitor` injection for live refresh subtitles.

## Why this direction
The scroll sample in #1674 suggests every scroll event can trigger expensive menu row layout/reconciliation work. This branch keeps the existing detailed Overview UI, but removes the most suspicious per-hover SwiftUI state propagation from Overview rows and makes hit testing/highlighting local to the AppKit host view.

This is an alternative to the lite-row PR; they are not meant to merge together.

## Tests
- `swift test --filter "overview row"`
- `swift test --filter "highlight"`
- `swift build`
- `make check`
- `git diff --check`

## Not covered
- I do not have a fresh macOS 27 live scroll profile after this patch. The branch is intended to give maintainers a concrete, test-covered option to validate against the lag in #1674.
target.author: hhh2210
